question: Under what condition may the Customer redistribute the software, and what is the maximum number of production servers allowed on the Standard tier?
gold_answer: Redistribution is allowed only with prior written consent; the Standard tier allows up to 8 production servers.
document:
ACME PLATFORM LICENSE AGREEMENT — Version 4.2

Recitals. This Agreement is entered into between ACME Software Limited, a company registered in England and Wales ('ACME'), and the entity accepting these terms ('the Customer'). It governs all use of the ACME analytics platform and any updates, and supersedes any prior agreement between the parties relating to the same subject matter. Capitalised terms have the meanings given in the Definitions schedule at the end of this document.

Grant of License. Subject to the terms herein, ACME grants the Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Platform. The license fee is billed annually and is non-refundable after 14 days. Payment is due within 30 days of the invoice date. The Customer is responsible for any withholding taxes arising in its jurisdiction.

Acceptable Use. The Customer shall not use the Platform to store unlawful content, to attempt to reverse engineer the object code except to the extent permitted by law, or to benchmark the Platform for the purpose of building a competing product. ACME may suspend access if it reasonably believes the Platform is being used to harm its infrastructure or other customers.

Tiers. The Standard tier permits deployment on up to 8 production servers and unlimited staging servers. The Enterprise tier removes the server cap. Downgrades take effect at the next renewal. Usage is measured by the ACME licensing daemon, and the Customer agrees not to circumvent it.

Redistribution. The Customer may not redistribute, sublicense, or resell the Platform to any third party except with ACME's prior written consent. Internal use across the Customer's wholly-owned subsidiaries is permitted without additional fees, provided each subsidiary agrees to be bound by these terms.

Support. Standard tier includes email support with a 2 business day response target. Enterprise tier includes 24/7 support with a 4 hour response target for Severity 1 incidents. Support does not cover issues arising from unsupported modifications made by the Customer.

Warranty and Liability. The Platform is provided 'as is'. ACME warrants only that it will materially conform to the published documentation for 90 days after delivery. To the maximum extent permitted by law, neither party is liable for indirect or consequential losses, and each party's aggregate liability is capped at the fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim.

Governing Law. This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales, and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of London.

question: How long should the patient avoid heavy lifting, and which medication should be taken with food?
gold_answer: Avoid heavy lifting for 3 weeks; take ibuprofen with food.
document:
PATIENT DISCHARGE SUMMARY — General Surgery Unit

Admission. The patient, a previously healthy adult, presented to the emergency department with a 12 hour history of right lower quadrant abdominal pain, nausea, and a low grade fever. Examination and imaging were consistent with acute appendicitis. Blood tests showed a raised white cell count. The patient was consented for surgery and fasted pre-operatively.

Procedure: Laparoscopic appendectomy performed under general anaesthesia. The patient tolerated the procedure well with no complications. Estimated blood loss was minimal. The appendix was sent for histology. The patient recovered in the ward overnight and was mobilising independently the following morning.

Activity. You may walk and climb stairs as tolerated starting today. Avoid heavy lifting (anything over 10 lb / 4.5 kg) for 3 weeks. You may return to desk work after 5 days if you feel ready. Do not drive while taking prescription pain medication, and check with your insurer before resuming driving.

Diet. There are no specific dietary restrictions. Start with light meals and increase as tolerated. Staying well hydrated and eating fibre will help avoid constipation, which is common after surgery and anaesthesia.

Medications. Take ibuprofen 400 mg every 6 hours with food to reduce stomach irritation. Paracetamol 500 mg may be taken between ibuprofen doses. A 5 day course of the prescribed antibiotic must be completed even if you feel well. Do not exceed the maximum daily doses printed on the packaging.

Wound care. Keep the incision sites clean and dry for 48 hours. You may shower after 2 days but avoid baths or swimming for 2 weeks. The dissolvable stitches do not need removal. Some bruising around the small incisions is normal.

Follow-up. A histology result will be sent to your GP within two weeks. If you were given a clinic appointment, please attend. When to seek help: contact the clinic or attend A&E if you develop a fever above 38.5 C, increasing redness or discharge from the wounds, persistent vomiting, or severe abdominal pain that is not controlled by the pain relief provided.

question: What is the total amount due including the early-payment discount if the invoice is paid within 10 days?
gold_answer: GBP 4,410 (GBP 4,500 subtotal minus a 2% early-payment discount of GBP 90).
document:
MERIDIAN ROBOTICS — SUPPLIER INVOICE PACKET

Cover note. Please find enclosed the invoice for services rendered in May 2026 together with the timesheet summary and the signed delivery acceptance form. This packet is provided for your accounts payable records; the binding figures are those on the invoice itself. Questions about the engagement scope should go to your account manager, while billing questions should go to the accounts team.

Project background. The engagement covered the commissioning of a robotic cell on the client's assembly line, including calibration, safety sign-off, and operator training. The work was completed on schedule over three site visits. A separate maintenance contract, quoted under reference MC-118, is not included in this invoice and will be billed monthly once the warranty period ends.

INVOICE #INV-2043
Bill To: Meridian Robotics Ltd
Date: 12 June 2026
Due: Net 30

Line items:
- Consulting services, 30 hours @ GBP 120 = GBP 3,600
- Onsite installation, flat fee = GBP 700
- Spare parts kit = GBP 200
Subtotal: GBP 4,500

Terms. Payment is due within 30 days of the invoice date. A 2% early-payment discount applies to the subtotal if payment is received within 10 days. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month on the outstanding balance. VAT is not applicable to this cross-border supply. The discount does not apply to any subsequent maintenance invoices.

Remittance. Please reference the invoice number on all payments. Bank details are provided on the attached remittance slip. For billing questions contact accounts@meridian-example.com. Payments by card incur a 1.4% surcharge and are therefore not recommended for invoices of this size.

Appendix — timesheet summary. Week 1: 12 hours (calibration). Week 2: 10 hours (safety and integration). Week 3: 8 hours (operator training). Total 30 hours, matching the consulting line above.

question: What port does the service listen on, and what is the connection pool max size?
gold_answer: The service listens on port 8443; the connection pool max size is 50.
document:
# Deployment runbook and production.yaml — order-service

## Overview
The order-service is a stateless HTTP service behind the shared load balancer. It reads from the orders Postgres database and writes events to the message bus. Scaling is horizontal; run at least two replicas for availability. Health checks hit /healthz every 10 seconds and the service is removed from rotation after three consecutive failures.

## Rollout
Deploys are blue-green. The pipeline builds the image, runs the test suite, pushes to the registry, and updates the green deployment. Traffic is shifted after smoke tests pass. Roll back by shifting traffic to blue; the previous image is retained for 7 days.

## production.yaml — service configuration
server:
  host: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8443            # TLS terminated at the load balancer forwards here
  workers: 4
  request_timeout_s: 30

tls:
  enabled: true
  min_version: "1.2"
  cert_path: /etc/ssl/service.crt

database:
  engine: postgres
  host: db.internal
  port: 5432
  pool:
    min_size: 5
    max_size: 50       # do not exceed; the DB caps total connections at 200
    idle_timeout_s: 300

cache:
  backend: redis
  ttl_s: 600

logging:
  level: info
  format: json

## Observability
Metrics are exported in Prometheus format on port 9090. Key dashboards track request latency percentiles, error rate, and database pool saturation. Page the on-call engineer if pool saturation exceeds 80% for five minutes, as this usually precedes request timeouts. Traces are sampled at 10%.

## Secrets
Database and TLS credentials are injected from the secret store at boot; they are never committed to the repository. Rotating the database password requires a rolling restart.

question: What was the primary endpoint of the trial, and by how many percentage points did the treatment group improve over placebo?
gold_answer: The primary endpoint was 12-week symptom remission; the treatment group improved by 18 percentage points over placebo (46% vs 28%).
document:
PEER-REVIEWED ARTICLE — Randomised controlled trial of Compound X in moderate chronic condition Y

Introduction. Condition Y affects an estimated 3% of adults and is characterised by relapsing symptoms that impair daily function. Existing therapies provide symptomatic relief but show limited durability, and a substantial minority of patients discontinue due to side effects. Compound X is a once-daily oral agent with a novel mechanism targeting the pathway implicated in recent genetic studies. Earlier phase studies established the 25 mg dose and an acceptable short-term safety profile.

Methods. We enrolled 420 adults across 22 sites in a double-blind, placebo-controlled design. Eligible participants were aged 18 to 70 with a confirmed diagnosis and moderate disease activity. Participants were randomised 1:1 to Compound X 25 mg or matching placebo for 12 weeks, stratified by baseline severity and prior therapy. The primary endpoint was symptom remission at 12 weeks, defined as a score below 4 on the standard index. Secondary endpoints included quality-of-life scores, time to first flare, and change in a serum biomarker. Analyses followed the intention-to-treat principle.

Results. Baseline characteristics were balanced between arms. Remission was achieved by 46% of the Compound X group versus 28% of the placebo group, a difference of 18 percentage points (p < 0.001). Quality-of-life scores improved more in the treatment group, and time to first flare was longer. The serum biomarker declined in the treatment group and was unchanged in placebo. Adverse events were mostly mild; headache (9%) and nausea (7%) were most common. Two serious adverse events occurred, one in each arm, neither judged related to treatment.

Discussion. The magnitude of benefit is clinically meaningful and consistent across pre-specified subgroups. Limitations include the 12-week duration, which does not address long-term durability, and the exclusion of severe disease. A longer extension study is ongoing. Conclusion. Compound X significantly increased 12-week remission versus placebo with an acceptable safety profile.

question: Which shipping method is free above what order value, and how many business days does it take?
gold_answer: Standard shipping is free on orders over GBP 40 and takes 3 to 5 business days.
document:
CUSTOMER HELP CENTRE — SHIPPING, RETURNS & FAQ

Welcome. This page answers the most common questions about orders. If you can't find what you need, our support team is available by email seven days a week and aims to reply within one business day. For questions about a specific order, please have your order number ready as it speeds things up considerably.

Processing. Orders placed before 2pm on a business day are dispatched the same day. Orders placed later, or on weekends and bank holidays, are dispatched the next business day. During peak sale periods dispatch may take an extra day, which we will note at checkout.

Methods. Standard shipping takes 3 to 5 business days and costs GBP 4.95, but is free on orders over GBP 40. Express shipping takes 1 to 2 business days and costs GBP 9.95 regardless of order value. International shipping takes 7 to 14 business days with prices calculated at checkout, and any import duties are the responsibility of the recipient.

Returns. Unused items may be returned within 30 days of delivery for a full refund. Items must be in original packaging with tags attached. Return shipping is paid by the customer unless the item is faulty or we sent the wrong item. Refunds are processed within 5 business days of receiving the returned item and are issued to the original payment method.

Exchanges. We do not offer direct exchanges; please return the unwanted item and place a new order. This is usually faster than an exchange and guarantees the replacement is in stock.

Tracking. A tracking link is emailed once your order ships. If your order has not arrived within the estimated window plus 2 business days, contact support and we will open an investigation with the carrier. Gift orders can be sent without a receipt if you select the gift option at checkout.

question: How many days notice are required to request annual leave, and how many days can be carried over to the next year?
gold_answer: Annual leave requires at least 14 days notice; up to 5 days may be carried over.
document:
EMPLOYEE HANDBOOK — SECTION 6: TIME OFF AND LEAVE

Our philosophy. We trust colleagues to manage their time responsibly and to plan leave in a way that balances their wellbeing with the needs of the team. Managers are expected to be flexible where they reasonably can, and to give clear reasons if a request cannot be accommodated. Nothing in this section reduces any statutory entitlement.

Annual leave. Full-time employees accrue 25 days of annual leave per year plus public holidays, accruing monthly. Part-time employees receive a pro-rata entitlement. Requests must be submitted through the HR portal with at least 14 days notice for periods longer than two days. Managers will confirm within 3 working days. During busy periods, teams may agree local blackout dates in advance.

Carryover. Up to 5 unused annual leave days may be carried over into the following calendar year and must be used by 31 March, after which they are forfeited. Carryover beyond 5 days requires director approval and is granted only in exceptional circumstances, such as leave cancelled at short notice for business reasons.

Sick leave. Employees receive full pay for the first 10 days of certified sick leave per year. A fit note is required for absences longer than 7 consecutive days. Please notify your manager as early as possible on the first day of absence.

Parental leave. Eligible employees are entitled to statutory parental leave; enhanced company parental pay applies after 2 years of continuous service. We also offer paid time off for antenatal appointments. Contact HR to discuss eligibility and planning as early as you can so cover can be arranged.

Other leave. Compassionate leave, jury service, and unpaid career breaks are covered in the appendix. Requests for unpaid leave are considered case by case and are not guaranteed.

question: At what oven temperature and for how long should the dish be baked before the feta is added?
gold_answer: Bake at 200 C (fan 180 C) for 25 minutes before adding the feta.
document:
MEDITERRANEAN ROASTED VEGETABLE TRAY BAKE — serves 4

About this recipe. This is a forgiving weeknight dish that uses whatever vegetables you have to hand. It is naturally vegetarian and can be made vegan by leaving out the feta or using a plant-based alternative. It scales up easily for a crowd; just use two trays rather than crowding one, otherwise the vegetables steam instead of roasting.

Ingredients. Two red peppers, one courgette, one red onion, 300 g cherry tomatoes, three tablespoons olive oil, two cloves garlic, one teaspoon dried oregano, salt and pepper, 200 g feta. Optional: a handful of olives, a pinch of chilli flakes, or a squeeze of lemon at the end.

Method. Preheat the oven to 200 C (fan 180 C). Chop the peppers, courgette and onion into rough 3 cm pieces and place in a large roasting tray. Add the cherry tomatoes whole so they burst and make a light sauce. Drizzle with the olive oil, scatter the crushed garlic and oregano, and season well. Toss to coat, and spread the vegetables in a single layer.

Bake for 25 minutes, then remove the tray, crumble the feta over the top, and return to the oven for a further 10 minutes until the vegetables are tender and lightly charred at the edges and the feta has softened.

Serve. Rest for 5 minutes before serving. This works well with crusty bread or over couscous or rice. Leftovers keep in the fridge for up to 3 days and are good cold in a salad the next day. To reheat, warm in a hot oven for a few minutes rather than the microwave to keep the edges crisp.

Tips. If your tomatoes are very sweet, a little red wine vinegar balances the dish. Firmer vegetables like carrots should be cut smaller or par-boiled first so everything finishes cooking at the same time.
